description: Anchor links should scroll smoothly to target section
do: Use scroll-behavior: smooth on html element

description: Fixed nav should not obscure content
do: Add padding-top to body equal to nav height
dont: Let nav overlap first section content

description: Current page/section should be visually indicated
do: Highlight active nav item with color/underline
dont: No visual feedback on current location

description: Users expect back to work predictably
URLs should reflect current state for sharing
Show user location in site hierarchy
do: Use for sites with 3+ levels of depth

description: Too many animations cause distraction and motion sickness
do: Animate 1-2 key elements per view maximum

description: Animations should feel responsive not sluggish
dont: Use animations longer than 500ms for UI

description: Respect user's motion preferences
Show feedback during async operations
dont: Leave UI frozen with no feedback

description: Hover effects don't work on touch devices
dont: Rely only on hover for important actions

description: Infinite animations are distracting
Some CSS properties trigger expensive repaints
do: Use transform and opacity for animations

description: Linear motion feels robotic
do: Use ease-out for entering ease-in for exiting

description: Stacking context conflicts cause hidden elements
Hidden overflow can clip important content
do: Test all content fits within containers

description: Fixed elements can overlap or be inaccessible
do: Account for safe areas and other fixed elements
codeGood: Fixed nav + fixed bottom with gap

description: New stacking contexts reset z-index
do: Understand what creates new stacking context
dont: Expect z-index to work across contexts

description: Layout shift when content loads is jarring
100vh can be problematic on mobile browsers
do: Use dvh or account for mobile browser chrome

description: Content too wide is hard to read
dont: Let text span full viewport width

description: Small buttons are hard to tap accurately
Adjacent touch targets need adequate spacing
Custom gestures can conflict with system
do: Avoid horizontal swipe on main content

description: 300ms tap delay feels laggy
Accidental refresh is frustrating
Tactile feedback improves interaction feel
do: Use for confirmations and important actions

description: Keyboard users need visible focus indicators
do: Use visible focus rings on interactive elements

description: Visual feedback on interactive elements
do: Change cursor and add subtle visual change
dont: No hover feedback on clickable elements

description: Show immediate feedback on press/click
Clearly indicate non-interactive elements
Prevent double submission during async actions
do: Disable button and show loading state

description: Users need to know when something fails
do: Show clear error messages near problem

description: Confirm successful actions to users
do: Show success message or visual change

description: Prevent accidental destructive actions
Text must be readable against background
Don't convey information by color alone
do: Use icons/text in addition to color

description: Images need text alternatives
do: Descriptive alt text for meaningful images

description: Screen readers use headings for navigation
dont: Skip heading levels or misuse for styling

description: Interactive elements need accessible names
All functionality accessible via keyboard
dont: Keyboard traps or illogical tab order

description: Content should make sense when read aloud
do: Use semantic HTML and ARIA properly

description: Inputs must have associated labels
do: Use label with for attribute or wrap input

description: Error messages must be announced
do: Use aria-live or role=alert for errors

description: Allow keyboard users to skip navigation
do: Provide skip to main content link
dont: No skip link on nav-heavy pages

description: Large images slow page load
Load content as needed
do: Lazy load below-fold images and content

description: Large bundles slow initial load
Repeat visits should be fast
Web fonts can block rendering
External scripts can block rendering
Large JavaScript slows interaction
CSS/JS can block first paint
Every input needs a visible label
do: Always show label above or beside input

description: Errors should appear near the problem
dont: Single error message at top of form

description: Validate as user types or on blur
do: Validate on blur for most fields

description: Use appropriate input types
do: Use email tel number url etc

description: Help browsers autofill correctly
Mark required fields clearly
Let users see password while typing
Confirm form submission status
Inputs should look interactive
dont: Inputs that look like plain text

description: Show appropriate keyboard for input type
Design for mobile then enhance for larger
do: Start with mobile styles then add breakpoints

description: Test at all common screen sizes
Mobile layouts need touch-sized targets
Text must be readable on all devices
Set viewport for mobile devices
Avoid horizontal scrolling
Images should scale with container
Tables can overflow on mobile
do: Use horizontal scroll or card layout

description: Adequate line height improves readability
Long lines are hard to read
Consistent type hierarchy aids scanning
Fonts should load without layout shift
Body text needs good contrast
do: Use darker text on light backgrounds

description: Headings should stand out from body
Show system status during waits
Guide users when no content exists
Help users recover from errors
Show progress for multi-step processes
Transient messages for non-critical info
Confirm successful actions
Handle long content gracefully
do: Truncate with ellipsis and expand option

description: Use locale-appropriate date formats
Format large numbers for readability
Show realistic placeholders during dev
Users should be able to skip tutorials
Help users find results faster
Dead ends frustrate users
Editing one by one is tedious
Users need to know they talk to AI
Waiting for full text is slow
do: Stream text response token by token

description: Elements should respond to eye tracking before pinch
UI needs Z-depth to separate content from environment
Video consumes massive data and energy
Heavy 3D/Image assets increase carbon footprint
AI needs user feedback to improve
Parallax/Scroll-jacking causes nausea
Icon-only buttons must have accessible names
All form controls need labels or aria-label
Interactive elements must support keyboard interaction
Use semantic HTML before ARIA attributes
Async updates need aria-live for screen readers
Decorative icons should be hidden from screen readers
All interactive elements need visible focus states
Never remove outline without providing replacement
Checkbox/radio must share hit target with label
Inputs need autocomplete attribute for autofill
Use semantic input type attributes
Never prevent paste functionality
Disable spellcheck on emails and codes
Keep submit enabled and show spinner during requests
Show error messages inline near the problem field
do: Inline error with focus on first error

description: Virtualize lists exceeding 50 items
do: Use virtual list for large datasets

description: Avoid layout reads during render phase
do: Read layout in effects or callbacks

description: Group DOM operations to minimize reflows
Add preconnect links for CDN domains
Lazy-load images below the fold
URL should reflect current UI state
Stateful components should support deep-linking
do: Enable sharing current view via URL

description: Destructive actions require confirmation
Use proper Unicode characters
Handle text overflow properly
Use non-breaking spaces for units and brand names
Never disable zoom in viewport meta
Avoid transition: all - specify properties
Never use outline-none without replacement
Use Intl for date/number formatting
Move await into branches where actually used to avoid blocking unused code paths
do: Move await operations into branches where they're needed
dont: Await at top of function blocking all branches
codeGood: if (skip) return { skipped: true }; const data = await fetch()
codeBad: const data = await fetch(); if (skip) return { skipped: true }

description: Execute independent async operations concurrently using Promise.all()
codeBad: const user = await fetchUser(); const posts = await fetchPosts()

description: Use better-all for operations with partial dependencies to maximize parallelism
do: Use better-all to start each task at earliest possible moment
dont: Wait for unrelated data before starting dependent fetch
codeGood: await all({ user() {}, config() {}, profile() { return fetch((await this.$.user).id) } })
codeBad: const [user, config] = await Promise.all([...]); const profile = await fetchProfile(user.id)

description: In API routes start independent operations immediately even if not awaited yet
do: Start promises early and await late
codeGood: const sessionP = auth(); const configP = fetchConfig(); const session = await sessionP
codeBad: const session = await auth(); const config = await fetchConfig()

description: Use Suspense to show wrapper UI faster while data loads
do: Wrap async components in Suspense boundaries
dont: Await data blocking entire page render
codeBad: const data = await fetchData(); return <DataDisplay data={data} />

description: Import directly from source files instead of barrel files to avoid loading unused modules
Use next/dynamic to lazy-load large components not needed on initial render
dont: Import heavy components at top level
codeGood: const Monaco = dynamic(() => import ('./monaco'), { ssr: false })

description: Load analytics and logging after hydration since they don't block interaction
codeGood: const Analytics = dynamic(() => import ('@vercel/analytics'), { ssr: false })

description: Load large data or modules only when a feature is activated
Preload heavy bundles on hover/focus before they're needed
Use React.cache() for server-side request deduplication within single request
dont: Fetch same data multiple times in tree
codeGood: export const getUser = cache(async () => await db.user.find())
codeBad: export async function getUser() { return await db.user.find() }

description: Use LRU cache for data shared across sequential requests
dont: Refetch same data on every request
codeGood: const cache = new LRUCache({ max: 1000, ttl: 5*60*1000 })

description: Only pass fields that client actually uses across RSC boundaries
do: Pass only needed fields to client components

description: Restructure components to parallelize data fetching in RSC
do: Use component composition for parallel fetches

description: Use Next.js after() to schedule work after response is sent
Use SWR for automatic request deduplication and caching
do: Use useSWR for client data fetching

description: Share global event listeners across component instances
Don't subscribe to state only used in callbacks
dont: Subscribe to state used only in handlers
codeGood: const handleClick = () => { const params = new URLSearchParams(location.search) }
codeBad: const params = useSearchParams(); const handleClick = () => { params.get('ref') }

description: Extract expensive work into memoized components for early returns
dont: Compute expensive values before early return
codeGood: const UserAvatar = memo(({ user }) => ...); if (loading) return <Skeleton />
codeBad: const avatar = useMemo(() => compute(user)); if (loading) return <Skeleton />

description: Specify primitive dependencies instead of objects in effects
do: Use primitive values in dependency arrays

description: Subscribe to derived booleans instead of continuous values
codeBad: const width = useWindowWidth(); const isMobile = width < 768

description: Use functional setState updates for stable callbacks and no stale closures
Pass function to useState for expensive initial values
do: Use function form for expensive init

description: Mark frequent non-urgent state updates as transitions
dont: Block UI on every state change

description: Wrap SVG in div and animate wrapper for hardware acceleration
Apply content-visibility: auto to defer off-screen rendering
Extract static JSX outside components to avoid re-creation
do: Hoist static elements to module scope
codeGood: const skeleton = <div class='animate-pulse' />; function C() { return skeleton }

description: Use inline script to set client-only data before hydration
do: Inject sync script for client-only values

description: Use ternary instead of && when condition can be 0 or NaN
Use Activity component to preserve state/DOM for toggled components
do: Use Activity for expensive toggle components

description: Group CSS changes via classes or cssText to minimize reflows
dont: Change styles one property at a time

description: Build Map for repeated lookups instead of multiple .find() calls
do: Build index Map for O(1) lookups
codeGood: const byId = new Map(users.map(u => [u.id, u])); byId.get(id)
codeBad: users.find(u => u.id === order.userId) // O(n) each time

description: Cache object property lookups in hot paths
Use module-level Map to cache repeated function results
do: Use Map cache for repeated calls
codeGood: const cache = new Map(); if (cache.has(x)) return cache.get(x)

description: Cache localStorage/sessionStorage reads in memory
Combine multiple filter/map into single loop
codeGood: for (u of users) { if (u.isAdmin) admins.push(u); if (u.isTester) testers.push(u) }

description: Check array lengths before expensive comparisons
codeGood: if (a.length !== b.length) return true; // then compare
codeBad: a.sort().join() !== b.sort().join() // even when lengths differ

description: Return early when result is determined to skip processing
dont: Process all items then check errors
codeGood: for (u of users) { if (!u.email) return { error: 'Email required' } }
codeBad: let hasError; for (...) { if (!email) hasError=true }; if (hasError)...

description: Don't create RegExp inside render - hoist or memoize
codeBad: function C() { const re = new RegExp(pattern); re.test(x) }

description: Use loop for min/max instead of sort - O(n) vs O(n log n)
codeGood: let max = arr[0]; for (x of arr) if (x > max) max = x

description: Use Set/Map for O(1) lookups instead of array.includes()
do: Convert to Set for membership checks

description: Use toSorted() instead of sort() to avoid mutating arrays
Store callbacks in refs for stable effect subscriptions
Access latest values in callbacks without adding to dependency arrays
do: Use useLatest for fresh values in stable callbacks
codeGood: const cbRef = useLatest(cb); useEffect(() => { setTimeout(() => cbRef.current()) }, [])
